articles.title: US strikes on Iran’s Hormozgan province kill eight people: Report
articles.summary: US strikes on Iran’s Hormozgan province kill eight people: Report Local Iranian media have reported that US attacks on Iran’s southern Hormozgan province over the past two nights killed “about seven to eight people”, all of them civilians, according to Ahmad Moradi, a representative for the province who spoke to Iran’s Tasnim news agency. Tasnim reported that one strike targeted a bridge in the province, hitting two family cars and killing six people. articles.title: Kuwait reschedules flights due to airspace closures
articles.summary: Kuwait reschedules flights due to airspace closures Kuwait Airways said it is rescheduling some flights due to airspace closures, according to a statement posted on social media. Similar disruptions occurred during the regional escalation earlier this year, when airspace closures across several Gulf countries caused widespread travel disruptions, affecting hundreds of thousands of passengers. Many travellers transiting through the region were stranded for days, and in some cases weeks, as flights were suspended or rerouted.

Iran’s Tasnim agency quoted Hamzehpour as referring to the US strikes as “a series of crimes and terrorist attacks”. This brings the number of Lebanese schools destroyed in Israeli strikes to 20. Unesco said last month that 17 schools have been destroyed and more than 100 damaged in Lebanon since Israel launched its military campaign. articles.summary: Report: Nearly a quarter of Israelis considering leaving if Netanyahu is re-elected About 23 percent of Israelis are considering leaving the country, or have a relative who is, if Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is re-elected, according to a Channel 12 poll reported by The Times of Israel.
